name: Snow Report 2026-02-26 16:00:55
report:
Evening, Beast fam, 

The skies opened into a wide stretch of blue as the sun poured down on our faces, setting the tone for an all-time day on the slopes with surfaces riding fantastically from top to bottom. We’re still sitting at 100 percent open terrain, offering more skiing and riding than anywhere else in the East.

Tomorrow will bring mostly sunny skies with highs near 29 at the base and 23 at the top along with breezy winds of 15 to 20 mph coming from the southwest. 

The snow had a great texture to lean into today. There was plenty of that perfect packed powder with more of a grippy feel making turns even more confident. The bumps have been fun to maneuver on trials like Skyehawk and in the woods, Twister and Squeeze Play were soft and a blast to rip as more intermediate friendly glades. Superstar was covered in that grippy and chalky snowmaking snow that is really fun to play with. Cruise Control once again delivered everything you’d want in a trail, the perfect pitch with reliable packed powder. Check out more of Needle’s Eye tomorrow if you haven’t been down there in a bit. If you’re looking to get into some woods that are more open and still skiing & riding well, Ramshead is your spot. 

Our grooming team is adding some big players to the plan in Ovation and Downdraft. Head over to Reason for fresh morning corduroy or Ridge Run for steeper carving and check out our Lifts & Trails Report to see the whole list of groomers. Our snowmaking crew has the same plan and will fire up the snowguns on Bear Trax and Superstar, with guns covering spots around the K-1 base, Mouse Trap, and Snowdon base.

Our key lifts are scheduled to start up at 9:00 a.m. The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m., while the South Ridge and Canyon Quads will spin from 10:00 a.m. - 3:30 p.m.

On the kparks front, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain, while The Stash remains in great shape with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for sweet progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature. 

The Beast remains on fire! 

– Evan

 

Operating Day: 108

Lifts     

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.    

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.     

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!     

Uphill Travel     

All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route.  

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. Please keep an eye out for grooming equipment and steer clear of mountain ops during non-operating hours.

name: Snow Report 2026-02-26 05:49:12
report:
Good morning, Beast fans, 

The slopes are still skiing and riding beautifully, with packed powder waiting around just about every turn. An early round of flakes dropped an inch of fresh this yesterday morning, adding a little extra sparkle to the surface. Pair that with the solid base our snowmaking crew has been building, and the mountain is staying in prime shape. We are still sitting at 100 percent open terrain with the most skiing and riding offered in the East.

A couple of light snow showers did pass through last night, leaving just a dusting of new stuff. Today starts mostly cloudy before the sun takes over. Temps top out near 18 at the top and 24 at the base with west winds around 17 to 22 mph making things feel a little chillier.

The snow surfaces here have been some of the best, with soft packed powder that loves to grab your edge and dish out smooth carving. The visibility has improved greatly, and our snowmakers have lightened up the guns to help out with that. Cascade was super enjoyable to rip down with grabby snow on a steep trail. Full House has been a great warm up run to build up the arcs and Needle’s Eye was holding fun and powdery moguls. Head over to Bear for consistent and fun riding or to the Canyon for some more challenging options. 

Our grooming team got after 79 trails with many of the same runs involved and Outer Limits plus OL Express added in. Be sure to get on that steep ripper while it’s smooth and primed for carving. Bunny Buster will also have corduroy that can be great for a mellow ride or wall to wall carving action, it's all up to you. 

Our snowmakers lit up Lower Bear Trax and Superstar, with snowguns covering spots around the K-1 base, Mouse Trap, and Snowdon base. They’ve been absolutely crushing it lately, moving the guns around strategically to make snow where the weather gives us the best window and the mountain needs it most.

Our key lifts are scheduled to start up at 9:00 a.m. The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m., while South Ridge Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. - 3:30 p.m.

On the kparks front, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain, while The Stash remains in great shape with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for sweet progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature. 

It keeps getting better, get out there!

name: Snow Report 2026-02-25 15:03:25
report:
Good morning, Beast fans, 

It was a bit of a chilly one out there yesterday, but visibility greatly improved after the morning, and the sun was shining through much of the afternoon. Today will bring a hike in temperatures back into the 20s, along with around 1-3" of expected snowfall during the day! 

Temps will start around 15° at the base and increase to around 30° by the end of the day. Wind speeds will be similar but in a different direction, pointing more towards the southwest at the base and at the peak. The increased temps mean exposed skin won’t be as much of an issue with any wind. Snowfall should start during the day and continue into this evening. 

Yesterday’s snow surfaces were fun once visibility improved during the morning. A few areas were somewhat affected by winds, but groomed surfaces were still extremely edge-able everywhere. I checked out North Star on Snowdon and Breakaway on South Ridge since both got a groom last night, and they were both in excellent condition. Breakaway should still be in fine condition to check out (and potentially lap) once the South Ridge Quad spins. 

Our grooming plan was much of the same, with many of our cruisers getting the corduroy treatment. The runout of Breakaway was cleaned up a bit overnight, as well as Flume and Idler. Ongoing snowmaking on Blue Heaven will be pushed out, and Lower Pipe Dream will provide the easiest route to the South Ridge Quad. 

Our snowmaking plan made some tweaks to adjust for the wind. Our crews will be shutting down all the snowguns that don't support Superstar and Skyepeak as the wind direction has shifted from what was forecasted. They aim to get back on Superstar to add more guns and intensity later today and will also turn on Upper Bear Trax. 

We’ll have 14 lifts to choose from, with all of our key lifts scheduled to start up at 9:00 a.m. North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m., while South Ridge Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. - 3:30 p.m. as a bonus Wednesday option.  

On the kparks front, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain, while The Stash remains in great shape with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for sweet progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature. 

See you out there for more midweek magic!

– Evan

 

Operating Day: 106 

Lifts     

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.    

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.     

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!     

Uphill Travel     

All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route.  

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window. name: Snow Report 2026-02-24 05:43:24
report:
Good morning, Beast fans!

After receiving some of the outskirts of yesterday's Nor'easter, today is lining up to be another prime midwinter day out there. Another 2" of snowfall gave us a slight refresh on surfaces and kept turns feeling soft yesterday.

Today trends colder than yesterday, with highs around 8 degrees at the summit and in the teens at the base. Visibility is pretty low to start the day, so make sure to control your speed while you're out there. A north breeze will start the day on the stronger side before easing back as the storm continues to move out. It’ll be crisp first thing in the morning today, so plan on that extra layer and you’ll be in great shape for quality laps.

Surfaces remain in a strong place. Packed powder and machine-groomed terrain continue to deliver smooth, edgeable turns with full coverage from top to bottom. With 100% of our trails open, you’ve got room to spread out. Long cruisers, steeps, trees, and everything in between are skiing well right now.

Our groomers got to many of their usual suspects overnight with the exception of Superstar Headwall, which will be removed from the lineup for snowmaking. Breakaway and North Star both got a groom overnight, so those trails should be running smoothly first thing this morning. Be sure to check the Lifts & Trails Report for real-time updates and to dial in your game plan for today.

Snowmaking targets remain focused on Upper Bear Trax, Superstar Headwall, sections of Great Northern down to Snowdon base, Mouse Trap, fan guns in the Superstar base area, and Blue Heaven has been added to the plan. As always, surfaces near active snowmaking may be a bit more variable, so stay alert in those zones. Blue Heaven is currently closed due to low visibility on the trail, as higher elevation locations are still seeing some lower visibility.

We have 13 lifts scheduled to spin today, with key lifts opening at 9:00 a.m. and the North Ridge Quad following at 9:30 a.m. 

Over towards Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain, while The Stash remains fully in play with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. Check our Terrain Parks Report for the lowdown on each feature.

Looking ahead, we’ve got additional snow chances lining up later in the week. Winter isn’t backing down, and neither are we.

Have a great day out there today!

- Cullen

name: Snow Report 2026-02-22 16:00:47
report:
Good evening, Beast fans,

We wrapped up a powder filled and gold medal winning weekend in style and we still have more snow to look forward to. On top of it all, the mountain is wide open, and we’re continuing to lead the East with every single trail ready to rip.

Snow returns tonight with steady flakes developing along with a few heavier bursts. We’re looking at 1 to 2 inches of fresh snow by morning, setting us up for a lil’ refresh to kick off the day. In the latest model run, it looks like the storm may be heading further south, but we have several more chances for snow in the coming week if it misses us this round. The storm could stick around through tomorrow, with several inches expected and highs in the teens at the top and low twenties at the base. 

Our key lifts are scheduled to start spinning at 9:00 a.m., except for the North Ridge Quad starting at 9:30 a.m. and the Canyon Quad at 10:00 a.m.

The mountain conditions have been on a hot streak, with nice soft packed powder all over. There are a few spots of hardpack forming in more popular areas, but grooming has plans for that. There’s also dense pow with untouched stashes if you care to venture into the woods. Double Dipper was dishing out nice turns today as well as more mellow cruisers like Upper Royal Flush and Skyeburst. Tomorrow should bring more packed powder and carve worthy machine groomed surfaces, and maybe even some freshies depending on how mother nature treats us. 

Our grooming added Downdraft and Superstar to their plan of smoothing out over 90 trails. Outer Limits will get another resurface and will be losing the bumps on the left for more space to arc, but don’t worry, they’ll be back in due time. Head over to Rime tomorrow if you're looking for one of the best places to carve. Check out our Lifts & Trails Report to see the whole list and plan out your sessions.

Snowmaking plans to charge things up on Upper Bear Trax, Superstar Headwall, sections of Great Northern from the top down to Snowdon base, and Mouse Trap, along with fan guns at the Superstar base area. This plan is subject to change depending on the wind levels coming from the East so stay tuned for updates. 

Over towards Bear Mountain, Dream Maker is firing with 21 features between the upper and lower sections, including a sweet rail section near the base of Bear Mountain, while The Stash remains fully in play with its wooden and stone features plus additional natural hits in the woods. On Ramshead, Timberline continues to shine with 45 features, 5 jumps, and a halfpipe, and Easy Street, Lil’ Stash, and Vale Yard are all dialed in for fun progression options. 

See you for another awesome week of shredding!

– Evan

 

Operating Day: 104 

Lifts    

Key lifts operate seven days a week from 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Monday through Friday, and from 8:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on Saturday and Sunday plus Peak Dates*; the Bear Mountain Quad will operate from 7:30 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. every Saturday through the end of March.   

The North Ridge Quad will open at 9:30 a.m. on weekdays and 8:00 a.m. on weekends, the Canyon Quad will spin from 10:00 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. Monday, Wednesday, Friday and 9:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m. on weekends, and the Sunrise Village Triple will spin until 3:00 p.m. on weekdays and 4:00 p.m. on weekends.    

A note for our snowboarders: Thanks to our new cabins on the Skyeship Gondola, we're asking you to please utilize the snowboard racks outside of each cabin. If the racks are entirely full, please put your boards underneath the seats. This differs from our K-1 Gondola, where all snowboards should be brought inside the cabins. Just remember: outside Skyeship, inside K-1!    

Uphill Travel    

All uphill travel routes are currently open! Please visit killington.com/uphill for specific route information, including a map of each route. 

Before heading up the hill, be sure to review our uphill travel policies and grab your Uphill Travel Pass here. All uphill travelers are required to have an Uphill Travel Pass and armband, which can be picked up at any ticket window.
